title: Q Groundwork part 2 of 2 announced
wikitext:
[]: Q Groundwork part 2 of 2 announced             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Sep 20, 2013 9:26 am http://www.evastore2.jp/news/2013/09/entry_666/ Also known as the book that will answer none of our questions because it is purely about the animation of the film and basically has no text. Just to be doubly clear Announced: アニメーション原画集 = Groundwork = movie disected frame by frame What we actually need (but still no word of it): 全記録全集 = CRC = lots of interviews and concept art

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Sep 20, 2013 9:35 am Well, at least now we know that the usual ballet of post-Rebuild movie books is still going with Q, maybe we'll hear about the CRC soon! Isn't that a 10th of October I see in the title of the article? They announce the release fairly late, no? By the way I don't remember : the 1.0 and 2.0 Groundworks also included unused storyboards and concept arts? If that was the case that could be an interesting sight should they continue the trend with Q!

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Sep 20, 2013 9:50 am Groundwork for 1.0/2.0 did not include any unused material or concept art. They're 99% animation and often both the first version and the corrections for whatever the x.xx iteration or even TV->NME. They feature no storyboards at all, the farthest back you go from animation is the layout on very, very few occassions.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Sep 20, 2013 9:57 am Merde alors.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Sep 20, 2013 10:47 am That's why I tried to kill any and all potential excitement with the first post Still, even if they reveal nothing the Groundwork books can be fun SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Sep 20, 2013 11:03 am It's from the fight against Sachiel no, when EVA-01 goes berserk?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 20, 2013 2:00 pm GROUNDWORKS??? YES!!!!! I've never read a CRC. I don't ever plan on buying a CRC simply because I can't read Japanese. Besides, working out a film's mysteries for myself is more fun for me anyway. The fan-translated interviews in the CRCs are just a neat little thing to have to embellish by love of Eva and my fandom of the filmmakers behind it. So, this Groundworks for Q announcement actually excites me so much more than a CRC announcement.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Fri Sep 20, 2013 2:03 pm Thanks for the update, been wondering about this, although I'm still missing the 2.0 Groundworks, with those 2 volumes and bloody pricing. FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: I can't read Japanese.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 20, 2013 2:08 pm Oohh... Maybe..... Maybe the pictures would be enough to sway me. But the Groundworks have more of an instant gratification for me simply because it's all pictures. Maybe after all of the Groundworks have been collected (I have both from 2.0, and 1.0's currently in the mail), I'll buy me some CRCs and dig up transitions on the internet, using their print-outs as bookmarks as I "read" through the books.

[]:             Quote Post by riffraff11235 » Fri Sep 20, 2013 5:54 pm ElMariachi wrote: It's from the fight against Sachiel no, when EVA-01 goes berserk?

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Sep 20, 2013 6:12 pm riffraff11235 wrote: That looks more like the Zeruel fight from NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by airman4 » Sun Sep 22, 2013 5:50 am Animation so class courtesy of legendary animator Mitsuo Iso

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Sep 23, 2013 1:42 am So the groundwork is a physical book, right? If so, does it just show some selected layouts and pieces of genga, or you can see the animation in movement with them?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Sep 23, 2013 8:50 am Dream wrote: So the groundwork is a physical book, right? If so, does it just show some selected layouts and pieces of genga, or you can see the animation in movement with them?

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Sep 23, 2013 11:09 am ElMariachi wrote: It's from the fight against Sachiel no, when EVA-01 goes berserk?

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Sep 23, 2013 1:22 pm UrsusArctos wrote: It'd be nice to see, say...Kaworu getting his head blown off by the DSS choker in groundworks. That would be cool

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Sep 23, 2013 6:44 pm Quick, sort of related question. How come Groundwork of Evangelion 1.0 is credited as "GAINAX" and not "khara"? I thought GAINAX had little to nothing to do with NTE.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Sep 23, 2013 7:05 pm It was a while before khara established their own publishing label of Groundworks (グラウンドワークス) The original run of Groundwork of Eva 1.0 book was published by Gainax. ISBN-13: 978-4903713182 It was eventually reprinted in 2011 under the Groundworks label. ISBN-13: 978-4905033028 Similarly the original 1.0 CRC was published by Japan Publications Trading Co., Ltd. (日本出版貿易). ISBN-13: 978-4903713175 The November 2012 re-release was under Groundworks. ISBN-13: 978-4905033011 The 2.0 CRC and Groundwork books were all by Groundworks.

[]:             Quote Post by distopia » Tue Oct 01, 2013 3:03 am http://neweva.blog103.fc2.com/blog-entry-2581.html Q Groundwork should be out in the end of December 2013.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Dec 06, 2013 10:19 am Pictures are up at the Eva store. http://www.evastore.jp/pc/article/Z0000107.html It looks like for the first time ever the book will have LOTS of color instead of just a few pages. Also interesting to see them printing out the 3DCG linework, but it seems to be in cases where the overlaying 2D effect is also present. Do want.
summary: []: Q Groundwork part 2 of 2 announced             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Sep 20, 2013 9:26 am http://www.evastore2.jp/news/2013/09/entry_666/ Also known as the book that will answer none of our questions because it is purely about the animation of the film and basically has no text. Just to be doubly clear Announced: アニメーション原画集 = Groundwork = movie disected frame by frame What we actually need (but still no word of it)
